text.content:
• 1am session: checked Telegram (nothing pending), reviewed all 3 open To-Do items - all blocked on Lok, none safe to advance solo. Ran production build to confirm app health: passed clean, no code changes needed.
• Lok then said to answer the pose-tracking Phase 2 open questions myself and keep going. Shipped Phase 2 auto-detect: frame-diff motion centroid (Canvas 2D, not ML) auto-logs court positions during Track & Record instead of requiring manual taps. Self-throttles off to manual-tap fallback if a device cannot keep up - answers the low-end-Android and cloud-cost questions without needing Lok’s input on either. Self-check passes, build clean, deployed.
• 5am session: re-checked all 3 To-Do items - still all blocked on Lok (Supabase migration remaining scope is classical-reader only, out of this agent's scope; pose tracking waiting on his real-court test of yesterday's fixes; shuttle-hit detection waiting on his architecture pick). Nothing new from Telegram. Build still clean, nothing unpushed. Spent the session auditing supabaseService.ts/AppContext.tsx for more instances of the write-without-readback bug class found 2026-07-15 - checked every save/subscribe pair, all are correctly wired (subscribeClubs, subscribeMyRealMatches, subscribeChallengesFor, subscribeMySharedConversations, subscribeEndorsementsReceived, subscribeClubMessages, subscribeLiveMatch, subscribeCourtSession). No new bugs found, no code changes made this session.
